resource.note: last monitored bed — assign to this patient

warnings: Penicillin allergy — avoid PCN-class antibiotics if sepsis path activated
Hypotensive (92/58) — avoid nitroglycerin until RV infarct ruled out
Patient refuses to lie flat — respect positioning, do not force supine

hypotheses.name: Acute Coronary Syndrome (STEMI/NSTEMI)
supporting_signals: Chest pain radiating to left arm, 35 min
Age 58, F, HTN + T2DM + smoker (multiple CAD risk factors)

hypotheses.name: Cardiogenic shock / large MI with hemodynamic compromise
supporting_signals: Refusal to lie flat (possible pulmonary congestion or pain-driven)

hypotheses.name: Aortic dissection
rules_against: Pain described as radiating to arm (classic ACS pattern), not tearing/back

hypotheses.name: Pulmonary embolism
rules_against: Diaphoresis + arm radiation more typical of ACS

immediate.action: Repeat 12-lead ECG NOW and place on cardiac monitor; door-to-ECG already breached at 12 min
immediate.reason: ACS suspicion high; SLA breached, cardiology decision depends on ECG.

immediate.action: Establish 2x large-bore IV, draw troponin/CBC/BMP/coags/lactate, give ASA 324mg chewable
immediate.reason: Standard ACS bundle; ASA reduces mortality, no allergy/GI bleed contraindication noted.

immediate.action: Move to last monitored bed; hold nitroglycerin given SBP 92; cautious 250 mL NS bolus and reassess
immediate.reason: Hypotension with suspected ACS — nitrates contraindicated; gentle volume support before pressors.

monitor.action: Continuous cardiac monitoring + repeat vitals q5min; serial ECG q15min until pain resolves or cath lab
monitor.reason: Detect dynamic ST changes and hemodynamic deterioration.

monitor.action: Serial troponin at 0 and 1–3h; reassess pain score and SpO2
monitor.reason: Confirm/exclude myocardial injury per ACS workup.

escalate_or_redirect.action: Activate cardiology consult NOW (on-call ETA 15 min); prep cath lab pending ECG
escalate_or_redirect.reason: If STEMI on ECG, door-to-balloon clock starts; cardiology decision needed.

explanation.summary: 58F with classic ACS presentation (chest pain + left arm radiation + diaphoresis) and concerning vitals (SBP 92, HR 118, RR 22, SpO2 94) on a background of HTN/T2DM/smoking. Multiple risk factors plus hemodynamic instability place her at minimum ESI 2, functionally ESI 1 if hypotension worsens. Door-to-ECG SLA already breached — immediate ECG, monitored bed, ASA, and cardiology activation are top priorities. Hold nitrates due to hypotension.

citations.signal: Chest pain + arm radiation + diaphoresis with age ≥40, HTN, DM, smoker
High-risk situation with severe pain 8/10 and concerning vitals

checklist.task: Repeat 12-lead ECG and hand to physician for read
Place on cardiac monitor in monitored bed
Cardiology bedside evaluation / cath lab activation if STEMI
Reassess BP/HR/SpO2 q5min; escalate to MD if SBP <90 or new hypoxia
Confirm reassignment of last monitored bed and resource plan

why_this_tier: Classic ACS presentation in high-risk patient (R-ACS-01)
Severe pain 8/10 with concerning vitals meets R-ESI-T2
would_downgrade_if: ECG clearly normal AND troponin negative AND BP normalizes spontaneously AND pain resolves
Pain reproducible on palpation with benign workup
would_upgrade_if: STEMI on ECG (→ ESI 1, cath lab activation)
SBP drops <90 or altered mental status (cardiogenic shock)
